Overall:
Granular activated carbon is most commonly used for the removal of RDX from contaminated groundwater.  Additiional technologies that have been demonstrated to be effective for RDX removal include: chemical oxidation, alkaline hydrolysis, electrochemical reduction, and UV irradiation. Ozonation and hydrogen peroxide oxidation are not generally effective for removing RDX.
